LAW AND MOTION 302, RE: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF'S "MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AND RELEASE" IS MOOT, AS DEFENSE COUNSEL REPORTS UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY THAT THE SETTLEMENT HAS BEEN PAID. NO SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED, AS NONE WERE SOUGHT IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-11-2023 Vacated

Motion To Enforce Settlement
Off Calendar Oct-03-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Plaintiff's Motion To Enforce Settlement.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff's "motion to enforce settlement and release" is moot, as defense counsel reports under penalty of perjury that the settlement has been paid. No sanctions are awarded, as none were sought in the notice of motion.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-10-2023 Continued To Oct-16-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-12-2022 Set For Trial On Feb-14-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
